Gainesville FHA Loans

FHA loans in Gainesville, Florida

Buy a home in Gainesville with as little as 3.5% down. Here's how FHA loans work in Alachua County, why the college-and-medical economy suits FHA buyers, and how to get pre-approved.

Gainesville Home Loans

FHA loans in Gainesville, Florida

Home to the University of Florida and a major medical hub, Gainesville has a stable, education-and-healthcare-driven economy and an affordable housing market. From historic homes in the Duckpond to family neighborhoods like Haile Plantation, FHA's 3.5% down helps first-time buyers, young professionals, and university and hospital staff become owners — with the statewide limit easily covering local prices.

Alachua County FHA limit (1-unit)$541,287

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Gainesville

With the 2026 FHA limit of $541,287 and FHA's 3.5% minimum down, you can finance a Gainesville home priced up to roughly $560,000 (your loan covers the price minus your down payment). In Gainesville's affordable market, that covers the large majority of homes for sale. A few Florida counties are set higher, so confirm Alachua County's exact figure with HUD.

FHA in a college and medical town

Gainesville's steady university and healthcare employment gives many buyers the reliable income FHA looks for, and local prices leave ample room under the loan limit — great for first-time buyers. Note that FHA loans are for primary residences, so they're for buyers who'll live in the home (not pure student-rental investments). Older Duckpond homes that need work can be financed with an FHA 203(k) loan.

Down payment help for Gainesville buyers

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Gainesville buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Alachua County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Neighborhoods we serve in Gainesville

We help buyers throughout Gainesville and Alachua County, including Haile Plantation, the Duckpond, and Northwest Gainesville. Wherever you're looking, we'll explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment — and get you pre-approved fast.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Gainesville (Alachua County) for 2026?+
Alachua County is at the 2026 Florida FHA floor of $541,287, which covers most Gainesville homes. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Can I use an FHA loan as a UF student or for a rental?+
FHA loans are for primary residences you'll live in, not pure investment rentals. If you'll occupy the home, FHA is a great fit; with a 2–4 unit property you can live in one unit and rent the others.
How much home can I buy with FHA in Gainesville?+
With the $541,287 limit and 3.5% down, you can finance a home up to roughly $560,000 — well above typical Gainesville prices.
How much down payment do I need in Gainesville?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score, reduced with gift funds and Florida assistance.
Do you serve all of Alachua County?+
Yes — Gainesville, Alachua, Newberry, and surrounding communities.
